slides.title: Why your energy *crashes after lunch*.
slides.sub: It's the post-meal glucose spike — and the acetic acid in vinegar may blunt it.

slides.title: Carbs hit fast
slides.body: A starchy meal floods your blood with glucose, then the crash drags down *energy and focus*.

slides.title: A spoon before carbs
slides.body: Take *one tablespoon of vinegar* in water 10–20 minutes before a carb-heavy meal.

slides.title: Acetic acid slows starch
slides.body: Acetic acid slows the enzymes that break starch into sugar, so glucose enters the blood *more gradually*.

slides.title: Steadier energy
slides.body: A gentler curve means fewer crashes — *steadier energy* and fewer mid-afternoon cravings.

slides.title: Dress it as food
slides.body: No shots needed — a *vinaigrette on your salad* before the pasta works the same way.

slides.title: Eat carbs *without the crash*.
slides.body: Snap your meals and Vibe Health scores how they hit your energy — so you see which tweaks actually keep you steady.

caption:
That 3pm energy crash? It often starts with the glucose spike from lunch. 🍝

One tablespoon of vinegar in water before a carb-heavy meal may flatten the spike — the acetic acid slows how fast starch turns to sugar. Steadier energy, fewer cravings. Save this. 🔖

Snap your meals and Vibe Health shows you which ones keep you steady → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why You Crash After Lunch (And the Vinegar Fix)
